audits.scope: Cardano ledger rules (Alonzo era) and Plutus integration — functional correctness review
audits.summary: Tweag reviewed the Alonzo-era ledger spec and Plutus script integration, finding no critical vulnerabilities but several spec-clarity and edge-case issues.

audits.scope: Cardano node networking layer and consensus (Ouroboros Praos implementation)
audits.summary: Well-Typed assessed the networking stack and consensus implementation, identifying minor issues in peer selection logic and no critical security flaws.

audits.scope: Plutus Core formal semantics and smart contract execution model
audits.summary: RV formally verified core properties of Plutus Core's reduction semantics, confirming soundness with minor documentation and spec inconsistencies noted.
Quviq applied QuickCheck property-based testing to the Shelley ledger, uncovering edge cases in delegation and reward calculation logic, all subsequently fixed.

audits.scope: Mithril stake-based signature protocol smart contracts and off-chain aggregator
audits.summary: Certik audited the Mithril protocol components, finding one high-severity issue in aggregator signature verification that was remediated before mainnet launch.

exploit_history.description: Minswap DEX (Cardano DeFi) suffered a front-running/MEV-style liquidity manipulation incident; not a protocol-level exploit but an application-layer vulnerability. No funds permanently lost.
MuesliSwap (Cardano DEX) experienced an order-book manipulation bug allowing partial order griefing; patched within 24 hours, no user funds lost.

Indigo Protocol (Cardano CDP/stablecoin) suffered a liquidation logic edge-case exploit where undercollateralized positions were not liquidated correctly during high volatility, causing ~$150k in bad debt absorbed by the protocol treasury.

dependencies.notes: Cardano DeFi relies on native oracle providers Charli3 and Orcfax rather than Chainlink (not natively supported on Cardano). Both are smaller, less battle-tested oracle networks compared to Chainlink/Pyth, increasing oracle manipulation risk for DeFi protocols.

Cross-chain bridges to/from Cardano (Wanchain, Milkomeda C1 sidechain, Celer) represent the highest attack surface. Milkomeda wraps ADA for EVM compatibility; bridge contracts have not suffered major exploits but are inherently high-risk. Wanchain uses MPC-based security.

Cardano is its own L1 using Ouroboros Praos (peer-reviewed PoS). No external L1 dependency. The protocol has operated without a consensus-layer exploit since mainnet launch in 2017 (Byron) / 2020 (Shelley).

Hydra Head protocol is Cardano's native L2 scaling solution, currently in early mainnet phase. Limited TVL and usage; sequencer/head operator collusion risk exists but is bounded by on-chain settlement. Still maturing.

rationale: Cardano's L1 protocol has a strong security posture underpinned by peer-reviewed Ouroboros consensus, formal methods (Agda ledger spec), and multiple independent audits with no critical L1 vulnerabilities ever exploited in production. The primary risk surface lies in the DeFi application layer, where immature oracle infrastructure (Charli3/Orcfax vs. Chainlink) and cross-chain bridges (Milkomeda, Wanchain) introduce medium-to-high risk comparable to other L1 ecosystems. The transition of stewardship from IOG to Intersect MBO (2024) introduces governance continuity risk that is being mitigated through on-chain governance (CIP-1694/Chang hard fork).
sources: https://rekt.news (searched — no Cardano L1 entries found)
https://immunefi.com/explore/ (searched — no IOG core protocol listing found)
